zh.Of, Storeton, Cheshire, England: 英國英格蘭爾斯托雷頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Bamville於1264年出生在英國爾斯托雷頓時，她的父親Philip de Bamville Lord of Stourton34歲，母親Nichola de Pulford28歲。她於1282年9月27日與William Stanley在英國爾阿斯特伯里結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1326年在她的家鄉享年62歲，埋葬在英國爾阿斯特伯里。

zh-hans.Of, Storeton, Cheshire, England: 英国英格兰爾斯托雷頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Bamville于1264年出生在英国爾斯托雷頓时，她的父亲Philip de Bamville Lord of Stourton34岁，母亲Nichola de Pulford28岁。她于1282年9月27日与William Stanley在英国爾阿斯特伯里结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1326年在她的家乡享年62岁，埋葬在英国爾阿斯特伯里。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bamville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Alban, Andre, Armand, Benoit.
French and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Banville in Calvados, France, recorded in the 13th century as Badanvilla ‘estate of a woman called Bada’. Compare Banfield .

variants.name: Banfield
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Banville in Calvados, Normandy, France. There has been some confusion between this surname and   Bonfield  . In Ireland this is a variant of   Banville  , the original French form of the placename, and now the usual spelling of the surname in Ireland; it is associated primarily with County Wexford.

English: habitational name from one or more of the numerous places in England with similar names, such as Bampfylde House in Poltimore, Devon and Bamville Farm in Wheathampstead, Hertfordshire. There may have been some confusion with   Benfield  .

variants.name: Banwell
meanings: English (Somerset): habitational name from Banwell in Somerset, named from Old English bana ‘killer’ + wella ‘stream, spring’.
English: habitational name from Banwell Farm in North Mundham, Sussex.
Irish (of Norman origin): variant of   Banville   or   Banfield  .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Bamville was born in 1264, in Storeton, Cheshire, England, her father, Philip de Bamville Lord of Stourton, was 34 and her mother, Nichola de Pulford, was 28. She married William Stanley on 27 September 1282, in Astbury, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1326, in her hometown, at the age of 62, and was buried in Astbury, Cheshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Bamville est née en 1264, à Storeton, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Philip de Bamville Lord of Stourton, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Nichola de Pulford, avait 28 ans. Elle a épousé William Stanley le 27 septembre 1282, à Astbury, Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1326, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterrée à Astbury, Cheshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Bamville 1264 in Storeton, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Philip de Bamville Lord of Stourton 34 und ihre Mutter Nichola de Pulford 28. Sie hat am 27. September 1282 in Astbury, Cheshire, England William Stanley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1326 im Alter von 62 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Astbury, Cheshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Bamville è nata il nel 1264 nel Storeton, Cheshire, England, sua padre, Philip de Bamville Lord of Stourton, aveva 34 anni e sua madre, Nichola de Pulford, aveva 28 anni. Lei sposato William Stanley il 27 settembre 1282, nel Astbury, Cheshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1326, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolta a Astbury, Cheshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Bamvilleが1264年にイギリスチェシュアイアストーレトンで生まれた時，父Philip de Bamville Lord of Stourtonは34歳，母Nichola de Pulfordは28歳であった。1282年9月27日にイギリスチェシュアイアアストブリーでWilliam Stanleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1326年に故郷で62歳で死去し，イギリスチェシュアイアアストブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Bamville(이)가 1264년 영국 체실르 스토레턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Philip de Bamville Lord of Stourton(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Nichola de Pulford(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 1282년 9월 27일 영국 체실르 애스트버이에서 William Stanley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1326년 그녀의 고향에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 체실르 애스트버이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Bamville nasceu em 1264, em Storeton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Philip de Bamville Lord of Stourton, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Nichola de Pulford, tinha 28 anos. Ela casou-se com William Stanley em 27 de setembro de 1282, em Astbury, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1326, em sua cidade natal, com 62 anos, e foi sepultada em Astbury, Cheshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Bamville родилась в 1264 г. в местности сторетон, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Philip de Bamville Lord of Stourton было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Nichola de Pulford, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Stanley 27 сентября 1282 г. в местности астбури, чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1326 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 62 лет и была похоронена в местности астбури, чешир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Bamville nació en 1264, en Storeton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Philip de Bamville Lord of Stourton, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Nichola de Pulford, tenía 28 años. Se casó con William Stanley el 27 de septiembre de 1282, en Astbury, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1326, en su pueblo, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultada en Astbury, Cheshire, Inglaterra.
